enriched_points.expanded_content: Computational models improve toxicity predictions by simulating interactions at molecular levels, allowing for accurate assessments of engineered nanomaterials (ENMs) under various environmental conditions.
In silico approaches decrease the need for animal experiments by providing reliable toxicity predictions, thus promoting ethical research practices in nanotoxicology and regulatory compliance.

Integrating computational methods with experimental data enhances the precision of risk assessments, allowing for better identification of toxicological endpoints and exposure risks associated with ENMs.
The future of nanotoxicology will focus on developing more sophisticated models, leveraging machine learning and big data to refine toxicity predictions and streamline regulatory processes.
